items.title: A ton of attention has been paid to future firsts the Bucks have traded away.
items.author: Jim Owczarski
items.body_excerpt:
A ton of attention has been paid to future firsts the Bucks have traded away.

In light of the Thunder-Hawks deal:

Milwaukee has traded away these 2nd Rd picks:

2026 (Jordan Nwora)
2027 (Pat Beverley)
2028-29 (Jae Crowder)
2030 (Andre Jackson Jr.)
2031-32 (w/ Pat Connaughton salary sweetener)

items.title: Asked Nneka Ogwumike about how emotional she was after the win: “I think it’s incredibly more meaningful, especially for this community of people that have been pouring into us, these legends that have laid the foundation, and I think I just felt it all in that moment.”

items.title: Only 18 male golfers have ever won two U.S. Opens. Three guys have done it in the last 10 years.
items.author: Jim Owczarski
items.body_excerpt:
Only 18 male golfers have ever won two U.S. Opens. Three guys have done it in the last 10 years.

Three players also won a pair in the 90s.

Two golfers have won three. Four have won four.

It’s insanely hard.

items.title: Asked Lynne Roberts about Nneka Ogwumike hitting the game-winner tonight: “I think everyone in our locker room wouldn’t have picked another person to hit that shot. ... That’s what sports can do is bring out those moments. But just you couldn’t have scripted it any better.”

items.title: Atlanta Hawks salary cap situation after trading for Aaron Wiggins into a trade exception.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Atlanta Hawks salary cap situation after trading for Aaron Wiggins into a trade exception.

They are now hard capped to the first apron, but aren't expected to exceed the luxury tax.

They could reclaim access to most of the $15 million mid-level exception by waiving Buddy Hield.

items.title: Aaron Wiggins projects to make between 5-6% of the salary cap next season.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
Aaron Wiggins projects to make between 5-6% of the salary cap next season.

And that number goes down the following year before a super-cheap team option.

(it's a very good contract and that's a big part of the calculus here)

items.title: RT @BobbyMarks42: Entering the offseason, Oklahoma City had a projected tax penalty of $213M.
items.author: Shams Charania Tweets
items.body_excerpt:
RT @BobbyMarks42: Entering the offseason, Oklahoma City had a projected tax penalty of $213M. 

The trade reduces that number to $152M. 

The Thunder enter the Tuesday Draft with two picks in the top-17 (no. 12 and 17).

items.title: Also, this:
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt: Also, this:

items.title: Obviously, there's still a lot to come for the Thunder, but trading Aaron Wiggins and taking no salary back creates a lot of wiggle room for next season.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Obviously, there's still a lot to come for the Thunder, but trading Aaron Wiggins and taking no salary back creates a lot of wiggle room for next season.

If you listen to @FrontOfficeShow, I've said for months that 2/5 of Dort, Hartenstein, Joe, Wiggins and K. Williams likely wouldn't be back.

items.title: The Oklahoma City Thunder will be ~$3.7M under the luxury tax, if they decline the team options for Lu Dort, Isaiah Hartenstein and Kenrich Williams.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
The Oklahoma City Thunder will be ~$3.7M under the luxury tax, if they decline the team options for Lu Dort, Isaiah Hartenstein and Kenrich Williams.

Thunder will be ~$11.7M under the first apron and ~$24.7M under the second apron before re-signing Dort, Hartenstein or Williams.

items.title: Man that's a real good move for the Hawks. I assume Isaiah Joe is next out the door for OKC as it looks to cut the tax bill. Somebody will likely be getting a good deal there, too.
items.author: Jared Dubin
items.body_excerpt: Man that's a real good move for the Hawks. I assume Isaiah Joe is next out the door for OKC as it looks to cut the tax bill. Somebody will likely be getting a good deal there, too.

items.title: Top #NBA prospects AJ Dybantsa, Cameron Boozer, Mikel Brown, Jr., Darius Acuff, Jr., and Kingston Flemings and their fathers talk about the #NBADraft, Father’s Day, life lessons, Black fatherhood and more exclusively with @andscape. tinyurl.com/yc6cs3bk
items.author: MarcJSpears
items.body_excerpt: Top #NBA prospects AJ Dybantsa, Cameron Boozer, Mikel Brown, Jr., Darius Acuff, Jr., and Kingston Flemings and their fathers talk about the #NBADraft, Father’s Day, life lessons, Black fatherhood and more exclusively with @andscape. tinyurl.com/yc6cs3bk

media.title: NBA Draft Prospects on Black Fatherhood | 2026 NBA Draft Preview | Andscape
media.description: Marc J. Spears sits down with top 2026 NBA Draft prospects and their fathers for a Father's Day conversation about presence, trust and the lessons that shape the next generation. AJ Dybantsa, Cam Boozer, Mikel Brown Jr., Kingston Flemings and Darius Acuff Jr. reflect on family, NIL, discipline, faith and what it means to have fathers guiding them from youth basketball toward the NBA stage.  Subscribe to Andscape for more sports culture documentaries, athlete stories, interviews, and original features exploring the intersection of sports, Black culture, and community.  #Andscape #SportsCulture #BlackCulture #NBADraft #NBADraftPreview #FathersDay #BlackFatherhood #AJDybantsa #CamBoozer #DariusAcuffJrVISIT  OUR WEBSITE: http://andscape.com SUBSCRIBE TO ANDSCAPE’S YOUTUBE: https://www.youtube.com/@Andscape_  FOLLOW US ON SOCIAL Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/andscape/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@andscape X: https://www.twitter.com/andscape  Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/Andscape  ABOUT ANDSCAPE Andscape is a Black-led media platform dedicated to creating, highlighting, and uplifting the diverse stories of Black identity. Magnified by the power and reach of The Walt Disney Co., the Andscape umbrella includes an editorial division, book publishing arm, film and television division, and music publishing group, each united by the shared mission of illuminating the culture and experience of Blackness. Founded in 2022, Andscape is a reimagination, expansion and diversification of The Undefeated’s former platform.

items.title: I mentioned a couple of times already this summer that the Hawks and Thunder were natural partners, including the potential that Atlanta could take either Wiggins or Isaiah Joe.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
I mentioned a couple of times already this summer that the Hawks and Thunder were natural partners, including the potential that Atlanta could take either Wiggins or Isaiah Joe.

As such, not a particularly surprising move but a quality role player on a good contract.

items.title: Nice little salary dump by the Thunder and gives OKC a little more wiggle room going into the offseason. Not just because of a smaller tax bill by lopping off Wiggins' $9 million salary but because it also opens up a roster spot. OKC now has room for one 1st round pick on its tea
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: Nice little salary dump by the Thunder and gives OKC a little more wiggle room going into the offseason. Not just because of a smaller tax bill by lopping off Wiggins' $9 million salary but because it also opens up a roster spot. OKC now has room for one 1st round pick on its team next season.

items.title: Source confirms the Hawks have agreed to trade for Aaron Wiggins.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
Source confirms the Hawks have agreed to trade for Aaron Wiggins.

Of note, Wiggins will be absorbed using the Luke Kennard trade exception.

Also, I'm told the trade may not be official until the moratorium is over so could be waiting a long time for an official announcement.

items.title: Wiggins -- drafted with the No. 55 pick in the 2021 Draft -- developed into a championship role player in Oklahoma City's culture and now moves to an up-and-coming Hawks team.

items.title: Final: Sparks 98, Liberty 97. Nneka Ogwumike scored 12 of her game-high 24 in the fourth quarter, including the game-winning 3-pointer as time expired, to lift the Sparks back from a 17-point deficit. Los Angeles is 8-8. They’re on the road at Toronto on Thursday. What a game.

items.body_excerpt: Final: Sparks 98, Liberty 97. Nneka Ogwumike scored 12 of her game-high 24 in the fourth quarter, including the game-winning 3-pointer as time expired, to lift the Sparks back from a 17-point deficit. Los Angeles is 8-8. They’re on the road at Toronto on Thursday. What a game.

items.title: Sparks are challenging the out of bounds call with 9.9 seconds left. Originally called Liberty ball. This is a massive one. Sparks would be out of timeouts if they lose the challenge in a game they trail by 1 (could be by 3 after this). We shall see.
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: Sparks are challenging the out of bounds call with 9.9 seconds left. Originally called Liberty ball. This is a massive one. Sparks would be out of timeouts if they lose the challenge in a game they trail by 1 (could be by 3 after this). We shall see.

items.title: It’s been a 19-8 run by the Sparks since falling behind by 14 late in the third quarter, and they’ve pulled themselves within 84-81 of the Liberty with 6:11 left to play.
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: It’s been a 19-8 run by the Sparks since falling behind by 14 late in the third quarter, and they’ve pulled themselves within 84-81 of the Liberty with 6:11 left to play.

items.title: Sparks trail Liberty 76-70 after three, but closed the quarter on an 8-0 run over the final 90 seconds. Los Angeles has five players in double figures, including Rae Burrell’s team-leading 14 off the bench. Sparks have trailed by as much as 17 tonight.
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: Sparks trail Liberty 76-70 after three, but closed the quarter on an 8-0 run over the final 90 seconds. Los Angeles has five players in double figures, including Rae Burrell’s team-leading 14 off the bench. Sparks have trailed by as much as 17 tonight.

items.title: The Sparks have rallied back and trail by just six points entering the fourth quarter.
items.author: Edwin Garcia
items.body_excerpt: The Sparks have rallied back and trail by just six points entering the fourth quarter.

items.title: The crowd for this Sparks-Liberty games has been incredible. There are fans packed even in the 300 section. This has to be the most fans the Sparks have played in front of at home this year.
items.author: Edwin Garcia

items.title: Hollinger POTD Richard Jefferson. Arizona SF was drafted 13th in 01 by HOU & traded to NJ, where he spent 7 yrs as key starter. Peaked at 22.2 ppg in 04-05. Bounced around several teams thru 2018 as useful vet. Okay shooter but bouncy & great at D. Won ring w/CLE. Good TV guy.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Hollinger POTD Richard Jefferson. Arizona SF was drafted 13th in 01 by HOU & traded to NJ, where he spent 7 yrs as key starter. Peaked at 22.2 ppg in 04-05. Bounced around several teams thru 2018 as useful vet. Okay shooter but bouncy & great at D. Won ring w/CLE. Good TV guy.

items.title: Lisa Leslie, speaking at halftime of Sparks-Liberty, honoring the 30 years of the WNBA: “We understood the assignment, because the assignment is not just to represent ourselves and our community, it was to represent every little girl who looked like us and wanted to be like us.”
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: Lisa Leslie, speaking at halftime of Sparks-Liberty, honoring the 30 years of the WNBA: “We understood the assignment, because the assignment is not just to represent ourselves and our community, it was to represent every little girl who looked like us and wanted to be like us.”

items.title: Halftime: Liberty 55, Sparks 43. Sparks shot 53% but had 7 turnovers converted into 12 points while the 9 New York turnovers resulted in just 4 points. Satou Sabally leads all scorers with 11 points; Kelsey Plum leads the Sparks with 9.
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: Halftime: Liberty 55, Sparks 43. Sparks shot 53% but had 7 turnovers converted into 12 points while the 9 New York turnovers resulted in just 4 points. Satou Sabally leads all scorers with 11 points; Kelsey Plum leads the Sparks with 9.

items.title: Kelsey Plum, who has been dealing with a lower left leg injury recently, has her calf area heavily taped. It was originally a white tape and has since been reinforced during the ensuing timeouts.
items.author: Justin Russo

items.title: Folarin Balogun, the US’s leading scorer in the World Cup, was born in Brooklyn, after his Nigerian parents, who resided in London, were visiting New York and his mother was deemed too far along in her pregnancy to board her return flight.
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt: Folarin Balogun, the US’s leading scorer in the World Cup, was born in Brooklyn, after his Nigerian parents, who resided in London, were visiting New York and his mother was deemed too far along in her pregnancy to board her return flight.
